segments.text: Now there were some present at that time who told Jesus about the Galileans whose blood Pilate had mixed with their sacrifices.
Jesus answered,
Do you think that these Galileans were worse sinners than all the other Galileans because they suffered this way?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you, no! But unless you repent, you too will all perish.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Or those eighteen who died when the tower in Siloam fell on them—do you think they were more guilty than all the others living in Jerusalem?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you, no! But unless you repent, you too will all perish.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then he told this parable:
A man had a fig tree growing in his vineyard, and he went to look for fruit on it but did not find any.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So he said to the man who took care of the vineyard,
For three years now I've been coming to look for fruit on this fig tree and haven't found any. Cut it down! Why should it use up the soil?
segments.speaker: Vineyard Owner

segments.text: 'Sir,' the man replied,
leave it alone for one more year, and I'll dig around it and fertilize it.
segments.speaker: Vineyard Caretaker

segments.text: If it bears fruit next year, fine! If not, then cut it down.
segments.speaker: Vineyard Caretaker

verses.title: Jesus Heals a Crippled Woman on the Sabbath

segments.text: On a Sabbath Jesus was teaching in one of the synagogues,
and a woman was there who had been crippled by a spirit for eighteen years. She was bent over and could not straighten up at all.
When Jesus saw her, he called her forward and said to her,
Woman, you are set free from your infirmity.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then he put his hands on her, and immediately she straightened up and praised God.
Indignant because Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, the synagogue leader said to the people,
There are six days for work. So come and be healed on those days, not on the Sabbath.
segments.speaker: Synagogue Leader

segments.text: The Lord answered him,
You hypocrites! Doesn't each of you on the Sabbath untie your ox or donkey from the stall and lead it out to give it water?
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then should not this woman, a daughter of Abraham, whom Satan has kept bound for eighteen long years, be set free on the Sabbath day from what bound her?
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: When he said this, all his opponents were humiliated, but the people were delighted with all the wonderful things he was doing.

verses.title: The Parables of the Mustard Seed and the Yeast

segments.text: Then Jesus asked,
What is the kingdom of God like? What shall I compare it to?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: It is like a mustard seed, which a man took and planted in his garden. It grew and became a tree, and the birds perched in its branches.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Again he asked,
What shall I compare the kingdom of God to?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: It is like yeast that a woman took and mixed into about sixty pounds of flour until it worked all through the dough.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then Jesus went through the towns and villages, teaching as he made his way to Jerusalem.
Someone asked him, 'Lord, are only a few people going to be saved?' He said to them,
Make every effort to enter through the narrow door, because many, I tell you, will try to enter and will not be able to.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Once the owner of the house gets up and closes the door, you will stand outside knocking and pleading, 'Sir, open the door for us.' But he will answer, 'I don't know you or where you come from.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then you will say, 'We ate and drank with you, and you taught in our streets.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But he will reply, 'I don't know you or where you come from. Away from me, all you evildoers!'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: There will be weeping there, and gnashing of teeth, when you see Abraham, Isaac and Jacob and all the prophets in the kingdom of God, but you yourselves thrown out.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: People will come from east and west and north and south, and will take their places at the feast in the kingdom of God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Indeed there are those who are last who will be first, and first who will be last.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: At that time some Pharisees came to Jesus and said to him,
Leave this place and go somewhere else. Herod wants to kill you.
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: He replied,
Go tell that fox, 'I will keep on driving out demons and healing people today and tomorrow, and on the third day I will reach my goal.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: In any case, I must press on today and tomorrow and the next day—for surely no prophet can die outside Jerusalem!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those sent to you, how often I have longed to gather your children together, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, and you were not willing.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Look, your house is left to you desolate. I tell you, you will not see me again until you say, 'Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord.'
segments.speaker: Jesus
